title: OpenVLA: An Open-Source Vision-Language-Action Model
abstract: We introduce OpenVLA, a 7B-parameter open-source vision-language-action model trained on 970k robot manipulation episodes from the Open X-Embodiment dataset. OpenVLA is built on a Prismatic VLM backbone that fuses DINOv2 and SigLIP visual representations, and predicts robot actions as discrete tokens in the language model's vocabulary. OpenVLA outperforms closed-source generalist robot policies such as RT-2-X (55B) in absolute task success rate by 16.5% on challenging BridgeV2 tasks, matches or exceeds performance on Google Robot and WidowX tasks, and enables efficient adaptation to new setups via LoRA fine-tuning with 100-500 demonstrations. We release model weights, training code, and a fine-tuning recipe to make powerful robot policies accessible to the community.
introduction_excerpt: Generalist robot policies that can follow natural language instructions and perform diverse manipulation tasks in the real world are a long-standing goal of robotics research. Recent work on vision-language-action (VLA) models has shown that large-scale pretrained vision-language models can be fine-tuned on robot data to achieve impressive generalist capabilities. However, prior state-of-the-art VLA models such as RT-2 rely on proprietary model weights and training infrastructure, making it difficult for the research community to build on these results.
